channel.title: Home on Stephan Bridger's Lab Notebook
channel.description: Recent content in Home on Stephan Bridger's Lab Notebook

item.title: Internet Service Providers
item.description:
Today I learned that some of the laws claiming to protect “free markets” in the internet provider industry are actually the reason I’m paying twice as much for worse service—and why I can’t switch.

In some locations, laws give a single Internet Service Provider an exclusive territory, blocking competitors entirely. As a counter-example, at my previous home’s address, various internet providers were allowed to service the area. Therefore, potential customers get to engage with a free market and pick the service that works best for them. At that particular location, it’s possible to get fiber optic internet service (500 Mbps) at just $45 per month. This outcome is only possible due to various companies being able to compete with one another.

item.title: Hello World
item.description: At vero eos et accusamus et iusto odio dignissimos ducimus qui blanditiis praesentium voluptatum deleniti atque corrupti quos dolores et quas molestias excepturi sint occaecati cupiditate non provident, similique sunt in culpa qui officia deserunt mollitia animi, id est laborum et dolorum fuga. Et harum quidem rerum facilis est et expedita distinctio. Nam libero tempore, cum soluta nobis est eligendi optio cumque nihil impedit quo minus id quod maxime placeat facere possimus, omnis voluptas assumenda est, omnis dolor repellendus. Temporibus autem quibusdam et aut officiis debitis aut rerum necessitatibus saepe eveniet ut et voluptates repudiandae sint et molestiae non recusandae. Itaque earum rerum hic tenetur a sapiente delectus, ut aut reiciendis voluptatibus maiores alias consequatur aut perferendis doloribus asperiores repellat.

item.title: About
item.description:
Once upon a time, I worked as a musician. But lately I’ve been building and taking apart other contraptions. I’m reorganizing my notes and configuring this new site. Consider it a lab notebook in progress. Previously, I’ve written elsewhere on the internet: GitHub, Blogspot, Tumblr, and Substack. But from now on, I’ll be posting here at lab.hexagr.am.

I’m interested in quantitative things like physics, math, and engineering. But I am also fascinated by the less tangible aspects of nature, like our lived experience, meaning-making processes, and social rituals. So, I find myself thinking about brains, language, and economics a lot, too.
